summary: Excellent front‑of‑house recording capturing a wildly inspired second set with Help on the Way, Slipknot!, and Franklin’s Tower soaring.
review: The show is praised for its frenetic energy and intimate feel, especially the second set where Help on the Way bursts out, Slipknot! ignites, and Franklin’s Tower becomes a joyous communion. The FOB recording puts listeners “right in the center of the action,” delivering crisp clarity that highlights Jerry’s melting solos, Brent’s stellar keys, Phil’s six‑string bass intensity, and solid drumming from Bill and Mickey.

ai_rating.rationale: Four detailed, high‑rating reviews highlight an outstanding performance and a clear, well‑mixed front‑of‑house recording; the lone zero‑star outlier is ignored as a data glitch.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑of‑house (FOB) capture with Sennheiser 441 mic; reviewers note crystal‑clear mids, an intimate “center of the action” feel and no significant dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as solid and classic for the era, while the second set feels organic, spacious and almost theatrical—building from a high‑octane Help on the Way into extended jams that maintain momentum through Slipknot!, Eyes of the World, and a powerful Other One.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring vocals on Franklin's Tower, melting fretboard work on Samson and Stella Blue, and effortless runs throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for spirited vocal moments and his six‑string bass adding intensity to the performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s playing highlighted as some of his best of the 1983 tour, contributing richly to the overall texture.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey provide a solid, jam‑focused foundation; Mickey’s lack of “beam” playing noted but overall drumming is praised for its energy.
